name: house-vs-senate
title: House vs. Senate
desc: A comparison of the two chambers of Congress. The House has 435 members serving 2-year terms with all seats up each election, a minimum age of 25, and represents districts by population; it starts revenue bills and brings impeachments. The Senate has 100 members serving 6-year terms with about one-third up each election, a minimum age of 30, and represents whole states; it confirms appointments and tries impeachments.
